start -> utente creato? — type=transition; label="utente creato?"; explanation="check if user is created"
utente creato? -> no — type=transition; label="no"; explanation="user not created"
utente creato? -> sì — type=transition; label="sì"; explanation="user already exists"
no -> createuser — type=transition; label="no"; explanation="user not created"
createuser -> utente creato con successo? — type=transition; label="sì"; explanation="user created successfully"
createuser -> no — type=transition; label="no"; explanation="user creation failed"
utente creato con successo? -> sì — type=transition; label="sì"; explanation="user created successfully"
utente creato con successo? -> no — type=transition; label="no"; explanation="user creation failed"
sì -> login — type=transition; label="sì"; explanation="user logged in"
login -> bacheca — type=transition; label="sì"; explanation="user logged in"
flow 1: start -> utente creato? -> sì -> login -> bacheca
flow 2: start -> utente creato? -> no -> createuser -> utente creato con successo? -> sì -> login -> bacheca
flow 3: start -> utente creato? -> no -> createuser -> utente creato con successo? -> no -> createuser -> utente creato con successo? -> no -> createuser -> no
this activity diagram models the process of user creation and login.
the process starts at the initial node "start".
it checks if a user is already created ("utente creato?").
if the user is not created, it proceeds to create a new user ("createuser").
after creating the user, it checks if the creation was successful ("utente creato con successo?").
if the user is created successfully, the user logs in ("login") and accesses the dashboard ("bacheca").
if the user is not created, it retries the user creation process until successful.
if the user creation fails, the process ends without logging in.
bacheca -> accesso a menu laterale — type=association; label="no"; explanation="access denied"
accesso a menu laterale -> menu — type=association; label="sì"; explanation="access granted"
menu -> visualizzare profilo? — type=association; label="sì"; explanation="view profile"
visualizzare profilo? -> myprofile — type=association; label="sì"; explanation="view profile"
visualizzare profilo? -> visualizzare i miei annunci? — type=association; label="no"; explanation="do not view profile"
visualizzare i miei annunci? -> myactivities — type=association; label="sì"; explanation="view activities"
visualizzare i miei annunci? -> eseguire il logout? — type=association; label="no"; explanation="do not view activities"
eseguire il logout? -> logout — type=association; label="sì"; explanation="execute logout"
flow 1: bacheca -> accesso a menu laterale -> menu -> visualizzare profilo? -> myprofile
flow 2: bacheca -> accesso a menu laterale -> menu -> visualizzare profilo? -> visualizzare i miei annunci? -> myactivities
flow 3: bacheca -> accesso a menu laterale -> menu -> visualizzare profilo? -> visualizzare i miei annunci? -> eseguire il logout? -> logout
this activity diagram models a lateral menu navigation process.
the user starts at the bacheca action.
depending on access, the user navigates to either the menu action or returns to bacheca.
from the menu, the user can choose to view their profile or activities.
if viewing activities, the user can also choose to log out.
the process includes decision points for accessing the menu, viewing profile or activities, and executing logout.
bacheca -> creazione annuncio? — type=control-flow; label="no"; explanation="negative branch"
creazione annuncio? -> createnoticeboard — type=control-flow; label="sì"; explanation="positive branch"
createnoticeboard -> creazione avvenuta? — type=control-flow; label="no"; explanation="negative branch"
creazione avvenuta? -> noticeboardcreated — type=control-flow; label="sì"; explanation="positive branch"
creazione avvenuta? -> createnoticeboard — type=control-flow; label="no"; explanation="loop back"
flow 1: bacheca -> creazione annuncio? -> no -> createnoticeboard -> creazione avvenuta? -> no -> createnoticeboard -> loop back
flow 2: bacheca -> creazione annuncio? -> sì -> createnoticeboard -> creazione avvenuta? -> sì -> noticeboardcreated
this activity diagram models the process of creating a noticeboard.
the process starts with an initial action ("bacheca").
a decision ("creazione annuncio?") checks if the creation of the announcement has occurred.
if the creation has not occurred, it proceeds to create the noticeboard.
another decision ("creazione avvenuta?") checks if the creation of the noticeboard has been completed.
if the creation has not been completed, it loops back to create the noticeboard again.
if the creation has been completed, it ends with the final action ("noticeboardcreated").
there are two distinct flows: one for when the creation of the announcement does not occur and one for when it does occur.
bacheca -> selezionare filtri?
selezionare filtri? -> no
selezionare filtri? -> si
no -> listnofilter
si -> filter
filter -> filtro corretto?
filtro corretto? -> no
filtro corretto? -> si
si -> listwithfilter
listnofilter -> viewnoticeboard
viewnoticeboard -> visualizzare dettagli?
visualizzare dettagli? -> no
visualizzare dettagli? -> si

flow 2: bacheca -> selezionare filtri? -> no -> listnofilter -> viewnoticeboard -> visualizzare dettagli? -> si -> noticeboarddetails
flow 3: bacheca -> selezionare filtri? -> si -> filter -> filtro corretto? -> no -> listnofilter -> viewnoticeboard -> visualizzare dettagli? -> no
flow 4: bacheca -> selezionare filtri? -> si -> filter -> filtro corretto? -> no -> listnofilter -> viewnoticeboard -> visualizzare dettagli? -> si -> noticeboarddetails
flow 5: bacheca -> selezionare filtri? -> si -> filter -> filtro corretto? -> si -> listwithfilter -> visualizzare dettagli? -> no
flow 6: bacheca -> selezionare filtri? -> si -> filter -> filtro corretto? -> si -> listwithfilter -> visualizzare dettagli? -> si -> noticeboarddetails
the diagram models the process of viewing announcements, starting with a selection of filters or proceeding without filters.
if filters are selected, they are validated before applying them.
the user can choose to view details of an announcement.
there are multiple paths depending on whether filters are applied and if details are viewed.
key edge cases include incorrect filters and no filter selection.
noticeboarddetails -> vuoi prenotarti? — type=association; label=""; explanation="decision point"
vuoi prenotarti? -> era presente un filtro? — type=association; label=""; explanation="decision point"
era presente un filtro? -> si — type=association; label="si"; explanation="yes path"
era presente un filtro? -> no — type=association; label="no"; explanation="no path"
si -> listwithfilter — type=association; label=""; explanation="path taken when filter is present"
no -> listnofilter — type=association; label=""; explanation="path taken when no filter is present"
listwithfilter -> booking — type=association; label=""; explanation="path leading to booking"
listnofilter -> booking — type=association; label=""; explanation="path leading to booking"
flow 1: noticeboarddetails -> vuoi prenotarti? -> si -> listwithfilter -> booking
flow 2: noticeboarddetails -> vuoi prenotarti? -> no -> listnofilter -> booking
this activity diagram models the process of making a reservation or viewing someone else's profile details.
the process starts with inputting noticeboard details.
a decision is made whether the user wants to make a reservation.
another decision checks if a filter was applied.
depending on the answers to these questions, the process leads to either a filtered list or an unfiltered list.
finally, the process leads to the booking or viewing of the profile details.
menu -> myactivities — type=control-flow; label="->"; explanation="flow to activity management"
myactivities -> gestione propri annunci? — type=control-flow; label="->"; explanation="flow to decision point"
gestione propri annunci? -> si — type=control-flow; label="si"; explanation="yes branch to display personal noticeboard"
flow 1: menu -> myactivities -> gestione propri annunci? -> si -> mynoticeboard
this activity diagram models the process of managing activities within a system.
the user starts at the menu and navigates to myactivities.
a decision point checks if the user wants to manage their own announcements.
if yes, the user is directed to the mynoticeboard to view their personal noticeboard.
there are no loops or repeated behaviors in the diagram.
menu -> myprofile — type=transition; label="->"; explanation="go to profile"
myprofile -> vuoi aggiornare dati? — type=transition; label="->"; explanation="ask for data update"
myprofile -> vuoi donare punti? — type=transition; label="->"; explanation="ask for points donation"
vuoi aggiornare dati? -> sì — type=transition; label="->"; explanation="yes to data update"
vuoi aggiornare dati? -> no — type=transition; label="->"; explanation="no to data update"
vuoi donare punti? -> sì — type=transition; label="->"; explanation="yes to points donation"
vuoi donare punti? -> no — type=transition; label="->"; explanation="no to points donation"
flow 1: menu -> myprofile -> vuoi aggiornare dati? -> sì -> modifyaccount
flow 2: menu -> myprofile -> vuoi aggiornare dati? -> no -> vuoi donare punti? -> sì -> pointsdonation
flow 3: menu -> myprofile -> vuoi aggiornare dati? -> no -> vuoi donare punti? -> no
this activity diagram models the process of modifying a profile.
the user starts at the menu and navigates to the myprofile section.
from myprofile, the user can choose to update their data or donate points.
if updating data is chosen, the user proceeds to modifyaccount.
if donating points is chosen, the user proceeds to pointsdonation.
there are two main paths: one for updating data and one for donating points.
the diagram includes a decision point for both data updates and point donations, allowing the user to choose whether to proceed or not.
created -> modify — type=transition; label=""; explanation="transition to modify state"
created -> booking — type=transition; label=""; explanation="transition to booking state"
modify -> finishtime — type=transition; label=""; explanation="transition to finishtime state"
booking -> accept — type=transition; label=""; explanation="transition to accept state"
accept -> accepted — type=transition; label=""; explanation="transition to accepted state"
accepted -> inprogress — type=transition; label=""; explanation="transition to inprogress state"
inprogress -> pointsassignment — type=transition; label=""; explanation="transition to pointsassignment state"
pointsassignment -> removed — type=transition; label="removed"; explanation="transition to removed state"
flow 1: created -> booking -> accept -> accepted -> inprogress -> pointsassignment -> removed
the state machine starts in the "created" state and can transition to either "modify" or "booking".
once in the "booking" state, it can proceed to "accept" and then to "accepted".
from "accepted", it moves to "inprogress" and finally to "pointsassignment".
the process ends in the "removed" state.
there are no branching conditions within the states, indicating a linear progression through the states.
main -> users — type=message; label="create user"; explanation="user creation request"
users -> db — type=message; label="map in database"; explanation="map user data to database"
db -> users — type=message; label="save data"; explanation="store user data"
users -> main — type=message; label="map data in users"; explanation="map user data back to application"
main -> users — type=message; label="notify user exists"; explanation="notify user existence"
flow 1: main -> users — create user
flow 2: users -> db — map in database
flow 3: db -> users — save data
flow 4: users -> main — map data in users
flow 5: main -> users — notify user exists
this sequence diagram illustrates the process of creating a user within an application.
the main flow starts with the main application sending a "create user" message to the users participant.
users then maps the user data to the database and sends a "save data" message to db.
db stores the user data and sends a "save data" message back to users.
users maps the data back to the application and sends a "map data in users" message to main.
finally, main notifies the user that the user already exists with a "notify user exists" message.
main -> notice board — type=association; label="create noticeboard"; explanation="create noticeboard action"
notice board -> db — type=association; label="map in database"; explanation="map data in database"
db -> notice board — type=association; label="map data in noticeboard"; explanation="map data in noticeboard"
db -> save data — type=association; label="save data"; explanation="save data"
flow 1: main -> notice board — create noticeboard
flow 2: notice board -> db — map in database
flow 3: db -> notice board — map data in noticeboard
flow 4: db -> save data
this activity diagram models the creation of a noticeboard announcement.
the main process starts with the main actor creating a noticeboard.
the noticeboard is then mapped into the database.
data from the noticeboard is mapped back into the noticeboard itself.
finally, the data is saved in the database.
there are no conditions or branches in the diagram.
main -> db — type=association; label="check if noticeboard exist"; explanation="check if noticeboard exists"
db -> main — type=association; label="return list"; explanation="return list of noticeboards"
db -> users — type=association; label="apply filter"; explanation="apply filter to noticeboard data"
users -> main — type=association; label="show noticeboard"; explanation="display noticeboard to user"
users -> main — type=association; label="booking"; explanation="user makes a booking"
flow 1: main -> db — "check if noticeboard exist" -> db -> main — "return list" -> main -> users — "apply filter" -> users -> main — "show noticeboard" -> main -> users — "booking"
this sequence diagram models the process of visualizing and booking announcements.
the main application (main) interacts with the database (db) to check for the existence of a noticeboard and retrieve a list of announcements.
the database applies filters to the announcement data and returns it to the main application.
the main application then displays the filtered announcements to the user interface (users).
the user interface allows the user to make a booking.
the diagram does not show any branching conditions or loops.
main -> db — type=message; label="check candidate list"; explanation="request to check candidate list"
db -> main — type=message; label="return list"; explanation="response with candidate list"
db -> users — type=message; label="search list"; explanation="request to search list"
users -> db — type=message; label="show candidate list"; explanation="request to show candidate list"
users -> main — type=message; label="accept candidate"; explanation="confirmation of candidate acceptance"
flow 1: main -> db — "check candidate list" -> db -> main — "return list" -> users -> db — "search list" -> db -> users — "show candidate list" -> users -> main — "accept candidate"
this sequence diagram models a process involving a main process, a database, and user interactions for candidate acceptance.
the main process initiates by checking a candidate list from the database.
the database returns the list to the main process, which then displays it to the user.
the user selects a candidate, and the main process confirms the acceptance of the candidate.
there are no branching conditions or loops visible in the diagram.
main -> db — type=message; label="check if noticeboard exist"; explanation="check if noticeboard exists"
db -> main — type=message; label="modify data"; explanation="modify data"
main -> db — type=message; label="return new version"; explanation="return new version"
flow 1: main -> db — "check if noticeboard exist" -> db -> main — "modify data" -> main -> db — "return new version"
this diagram models a sequence of interactions between an application (main) and a database (db) for modifying an announcement.
the application checks if a noticeboard exists in the database.
if it does, the database modifies the data.
finally, the database returns the new version of the announcement to the application.
there are no conditions or branches in this sequence.
main -> db — type=message; label="check if user exist"; explanation="check if user exist"
flow 1: main -> db — "check if user exist" -> db -> main — "modify data" -> main -> db — "return new version"
this diagram models a sequence of interactions between an application (main) and a database (db) for modifying a profile.
the application checks if a user exists in the database.
if the user exists, the database modifies the data.
the modified data is then returned to the application.
the process is straightforward and linear, with no branching or looping conditions visible.
main -> db — type=association; label="if noticeboard's period finished"; explanation="check if noticeboard's period has finished"
db -> main — type=association; label="delete noticeboard"; explanation="delete noticeboard if condition is met"
flow 1: main -> db — "if noticeboard's period finished" -> db -> main — "delete noticeboard" -> main
this activity diagram models the process of checking if a noticeboard's period has finished and deleting it if so.
the main flow starts with the main process checking the noticeboard's period.
if the period has finished, the db process deletes the noticeboard.
the process then returns to the main process, indicating the completion of the action.
there are no conditions or loops visible in the diagram.
